name: Calyptomenidae
description: Calyptomenidae is a family of broadbills comprising the African broadbills and the Asian green broadbills. They were formerly included in Eurylaimidae but are now treated as a separate family.

children.name: Smithornis
children.description: Smithornis is a genus of broadbills native to sub-Saharan Africa, known for their brownish, streaked plumage and prolonged song flights.

children.name: Smithornis capensis
children.description: The African broadbill is a brownish, streaked bird found in dense woodland and forest edges across much of sub-Saharan Africa. It performs a distinctive butterfly-like display flight while singing.

children.name: Smithornis rufolateralis
children.description: The rufous-sided broadbill has distinctive rufous flanks and is found in West and Central African rainforests. It is often detected by its loud, buzzing song.

children.name: Smithornis viridis
children.description: This species is a vivid green broadbill, distinguished by its striking pattern of black and white wings. It inhabits the forests of southern Africa.

children.name: Calyptomena
children.description: Calyptomena is a genus of brilliant green broadbills endemic to Southeast Asia, with males having hidden black patches on the wings.

children.name: Calyptomena viridis
children.description: The green broadbill is a plump, emerald-green bird found in lowland and hill forests of Southeast Asia. Its plumage blends perfectly with the forest canopy.

children.name: Calyptomena hosei
children.description: Hose's Broadbill is a green broadbill endemic to the mountains of Borneo, named after the English naturalist Charles Hose. It is smaller than Whitehead's Broadbill with a plain green throat and lacks the blue patch. It inhabits montane forests above 900 metres and is often found in mixed-species foraging flocks in the canopy.

children.name: Calyptomena whiteheadi
children.description: Whitehead's broadbill is a large green broadbill endemic to the mountains of Borneo. It has a distinctive blue patch on the throat and black spots on the wing coverts.
